heading: Denial of equal access prohibited
label: 4071 Denial of equal access prohibited
notes: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–377, title VIII, § 80198 Stat. 1302Aug. 11, 1984“This title [enacting this subchapter] may be cited as ‘The Equal Access Act’.”
amendments: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–377, title VIII, § 80198 Stat. 1302Aug. 11, 1984“This title [enacting this subchapter] may be cited as ‘The Equal Access Act’.”

children.heading: Restriction of limited open forum on basis of religious, political, philosophical, or other speech content prohibited
children.label: a Restriction of limited open forum on basis of religious, political, philosophical, or other speech content prohibited
children.text: It shall be unlawful for any public secondary school which receives Federal financial assistance and which has a limited open forum to deny equal access or a fair opportunity to, or discriminate against, any students who wish to conduct a meeting within that limited open forum on the basis of the religious, political, philosophical, or other content of the speech at such meetings.

children.heading: “Limited open forum” defined
children.label: b “Limited open forum” defined
children.text: A public secondary school has a limited open forum whenever such school grants an offering to or opportunity for one or more noncurriculum related student groups to meet on school premises during noninstructional time.
the meeting is voluntary and student-initiated;
there is no sponsorship of the meeting by the school, the government, or its agents or employees;
employees or agents of the school or government are present at religious meetings only in a nonparticipatory capacity;
the meeting does not materially and substantially interfere with the orderly conduct of educational activities within the school; and
nonschool persons may not direct, conduct, control, or regularly attend activities of student groups.

children.heading: Construction of subchapter with respect to certain rights
children.label: d Construction of subchapter with respect to certain rights

children.text: to influence the form or content of any prayer or other religious activity;
to require any person to participate in prayer or other religious activity;
to expend public funds beyond the incidental cost of providing the space for student-initiated meetings;
to compel any school agent or employee to attend a school meeting if the content of the speech at the meeting is contrary to the beliefs of the agent or employee;
to sanction meetings that are otherwise unlawful;
to limit the rights of groups of students which are not of a specified numerical size; or
to abridge the constitutional rights of any person.

children.heading: Federal financial assistance to schools unaffected
children.label: e Federal financial assistance to schools unaffected
children.text: Notwithstanding the availability of any other remedy under the Constitution or the laws of the United States, nothing in this subchapter shall be construed to authorize the United States to deny or withhold Federal financial assistance to any school.

children.heading: Authority of schools with respect to order, discipline, well-being, and attendance concerns
children.label: f Authority of schools with respect to order, discipline, well-being, and attendance concerns
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed to limit the authority of the school, its agents or employees, to maintain order and discipline on school premises, to protect the well-being of students and faculty, and to assure that attendance of students at meetings is voluntary.
